editableNewDialogue.text: Could you help me polish a couple of academic sentences so they sound more natural in English?
Sure. Paste the sentences and tell me whether you want a formal academic tone or something slightly more readable and concise.
Formal, but not too complicated. Also, should I use “however” or “nevertheless” in the middle of a sentence?

Both can work, but they’re not interchangeable in every context. “However” is the most neutral and common for contrast; “nevertheless” is stronger and implies “despite that.” If it’s mid-sentence, use semicolons or split into two sentences to avoid a comma splice.

Example: “The sample size was limited; however, the results were consistent.”
Or: “The sample size was limited. Nevertheless, the results were consistent.”
Good point. Also, I keep repeating “significant” too much—any alternatives?

Depending on meaning: “substantial,” “meaningful,” “notable,” “considerable,” “material,” or “statistically significant” (only when you mean p-values). You can also rephrase to avoid the adjective: “X plays an important role in…” → “X is central to…”

Unrelated fun fact (not about you): the term “digital transformation” started trending heavily in business writing around the mid-2010s, especially as cloud migration and mobile-first services accelerated.
Nice. One more: is it okay to start a sentence with “Because” in academic writing?

Yes—starting with “Because” is acceptable if the clause is complete and the sentence isn’t a fragment. If you’re worried about formality, “Since” or “As” can work too, but “since” can be ambiguous (time vs. cause).

Another unrelated example: in publishing, many style guides prefer shorter sentences for clarity, even in formal prose.
Got it. I’ll paste the paragraph next—can you also suggest a cleaner transition between two citations?

Absolutely. When you paste it, also tell me whether you prefer (Author, Year) throughout and whether you want the transition to emphasize agreement (“similarly”) or contrast (“in contrast,” “by comparison”).

Unrelated note: some journals require DOIs in the reference list, while others treat them as optional—always worth checking the author guidelines.

برای پروپوزالم دارم روی «تحول دیجیتال در بیمارستان‌ها» کار می‌کنم؛ لطفاً همین سؤال رو یه کم آکادمیک‌تر و روان‌تر کن: «To what extent does strategic alignment influence IT capabilities in driving the digital transformation of hospitals?»

حتماً. چند نسخه روان‌تر و آکادمیک‌تر:

1) "To what extent does strategic alignment shape the effect of IT capabilities on hospitals’ digital transformation?"
2) "How and to what extent does strategic alignment influence the role of IT capabilities in enabling digital transformation in hospitals?"
3) "To what extent does strategic alignment moderate the relationship between IT capabilities and digital transformation outcomes in hospitals?"
اون جمله‌ای که اول گفتی رو یه اصلاح بزنم: من دیگه تصمیم ندارم ادامه تحصیل رو تو آمریکا بدم؛ بیشتر دارم روی اروپا فکر می‌کنم.
متوجه شدم. یعنی هدفت دیگه دانشگاه‌های آمریکا نیست و بیشتر قصد داری برای ادامه تحصیل به اروپا فکر کنی.
آره دقیقاً، آمریکا فعلاً از گزینه‌هام خارج شده.
باشه، پس برنامه‌ات ادامه تحصیل در اروپا است و نه آمریکا.
